rationale: Traced the delta: the new loop `for _, c := range coveredRefuted { if c.Slot == "" || c.Round < 1 { return err } }` runs after decodeRefutations, before the len(pending)==0 early return, and only rejects a shape-invalid covering entry. For a valid bead, every covering entry written by writePanelRefutedMetadata always has non-empty Slot and Round>=1 (see the settle path in reconcilePendingRefutations, which builds toSatisfy from uncovered pending entries that are themselves shape-validated), so the loop is a structural no-op on valid inputs: no error, coveredRefuted/covered map/uncovered slice all identical to pre-fix behavior. reconcilePendingRefutations calls uncoveredPendingObligations directly and only consumes its `uncovered` return value to build toSatisfy for writePanelRefutedMetadata -- unaffected by this validation. Ran `go test -count=1 -v -run 'TestPanelRefuted_|TestPendingObligationPredicate|TestReconcilePendingRefutations' ./internal/complete/...`: all TestPanelRefuted_* subtests pass unchanged (same names, same assertions), and the 20-subtest TestPendingObligationPredicate passes including the new valid-covered no-false-refuse pin. Also ran `go build ./...` and `go test -count=1 ./internal/complete ./internal/lifecycle ./internal/executor ./internal/doctor ./cmd/mindspec` -- all green. No existing test expectation was edited; delta is purely additive (loop + 4 subtests). No reconcile behavior change on valid input.
